title: About me - James Murray for Ealing North
text_excerpt: About me - James Murray for Ealing North Skip to main content Homepage My priorities Get in touch About me More Menu About me Debating in the Chamber About James James was first elected as Labour and Co-operative MP for Ealing North in December 2019. In his first term he served as a member of the House of Commons Health and Social Care Select Committee, an Opposition Whip, and a shadow Treasury minister. James was re-elected in July 2024 and he is currently the Chief Secretary to the Treasury. James at His Majesty's Treasury Before becoming an MP, James Murray was the Deputy Mayor for Housing at City Hall, where he oversaw the Mayor’s £4.8 billion affordable homes programme. This included ‘Building Council Homes for Londoners’ – the first-ever City Hall initiative dedicated to building new council homes – which saw more council homes being built in London than in any time for the last 30 years. New council homes at Copley Close James grew up in Ealing with his brother and mum who was a Labour Councillor in Cleveland (now Pitshanger) from 2014-18. Election Night 2019 Further details Promoted by and on behalf of James Murray, all at Ruskin Hall, 16 Church Rd, London W3 8PP ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved.
